qa_pairs.question: When running Ansys Workbench jobs using a Slurm script, how can one prevent the solution from being overwritten upon successful job completion, particularly when conducting scaling tests on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To prevent the solution from being overwritten, change `Save(Overwrite=True)` to `Save(Overwrite=False)` in the last line of the Workbench Slurm script. This modification makes it easier to evaluate how well the simulation scales when increasing the number of tasks, as the initialized solution will not be affected by each test run.

qa_pairs.question: How is an input file for Ansys Mechanical APDL generated from an interactive Workbench Mechanical session on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: An input file for Ansys Mechanical APDL can be generated from within your interactive Workbench Mechanical session by clicking `Solution -> Tools -> Write Input Files`. You should then specify the `File name:` as `YOURAPDLFILE.inp` and select `APDL Input Files (*.inp)` as the `Save as type:`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the command-line syntax for running an Ansys Mechanical APDL job in Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) mode within a Slurm batch script on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The command to run an Ansys Mechanical APDL job in Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) mode is `mapdl -smp -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`. This command should be placed in a Slurm batch script after loading the necessary modules and creating an output directory.
